title: epipactis latifolia subsp. muelleri
intro: Epipactis latifolia subsp. muelleri, also known as the green helleborine, is a rare orchid species endemic to Australia, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and distinctive leaf shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Epipactis latifolia subsp. muelleri are thought to include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots and leaves, and can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions.
